name: Calculus
description: Limits of functions, derivatives, integrals, differential equations and their applications.
label: KAL

content.text: Calculus (mathematical analysis) is a branch of mathematics dealing with change and motion. Its two main pillars are differential calculus (derivatives) and integral calculus (integrals), linked by the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus.

A limit describes the behavior of a function as its argument approaches a certain value. It is the foundational concept on which both derivatives and integrals rest. A limit exists if the function approaches the same value from both the left and the right.

A derivative is the instantaneous rate of change of a function — think of a car's speedometer showing your exact speed at a given moment. Geometrically, the derivative is the slope of the tangent line to the graph of the function at a given point.

The integral is the inverse operation of the derivative. An indefinite integral finds the antiderivative, while a definite integral computes the area under a curve. Integrals have broad applications in physics, engineering, and economics.
